decisions.decision: Anchor health uptime to a module-scoped process-start timestamp captured when `backend/server.py` imports, and keep it outside `reset_state()` so `uptime_seconds` reflects the lifetime of the imported server module across direct router tests and direct `HTTPServer(..., BayesHandler)` usage.
alternatives_rejected: Capture the start time inside `run_server()` and miss direct-module and direct-router code paths.
Reset the health clock from `reset_state()` alongside domain state.
Use first successful request time, which measures traffic rather than process startup.

decisions.decision: Build component health through small shared helpers that always emit `status` and freeze the nested keys as `db: {status, kind}`, `inference: {status, backend, version}`, and `auth: {status, requires_agent_id}`, sourcing auth and inference values from the live in-process globals already used by the backend rather than re-reading environment variables per request.
alternatives_rejected: Flatten `components` to plain status strings without per-component objects.
Use different nested field names such as `store` or `store_kind` for the DB entry.
Re-read environment or config sources on every health request instead of mirroring runtime state.

decisions.decision: Represent the current checkout's `db` component as the in-memory state store with `kind: "in_memory"` and steady-state `status: "ok"`, rather than inventing an absent external database dependency or surfacing a placeholder degraded state.
alternatives_rejected: Report `db` as permanently `degraded` because there is no external database adapter.
Add a fake database probe for infrastructure that does not exist in this repo.

decisions.decision: Add a reusable top-level health-status reducer that uses the shared `ok` / `degraded` / `unhealthy` vocabulary, but keep the current component builders returning `ok` in this all-in-memory checkout so the aggregator is future-proof without changing today's steady-state payload.
alternatives_rejected: Leave top-level status hard-coded and skip a shared reduction helper.
Encode auth optional vs required as different status strings instead of sibling fields.
Constrain the helper to only emit `ok` and defer degraded or unhealthy support entirely.

decisions.decision: Compose the new `/v1/health` response with a dedicated shared payload helper that keeps the payload flat and top-level, reuses the existing minimal health fields, and appends the parent-planned `version`, `uptime_seconds`, and `components` additions from shared helper output rather than route-local dict literals.
alternatives_rejected: Wrap the new health payload in a `{..., meta}` envelope.
Build the `/v1/health` contract inline inside the router without shared helpers.
Split health assembly across several unrelated route-local literals.

steps.task: Add a module-level process-start timestamp near the import-time runtime constants in `backend/server.py` and implement a helper that converts it into float `uptime_seconds` at request time without touching `reset_state()`.

Implement shared component-builder helpers for `db`, `inference`, and `auth`, each returning the frozen nested object shape with `status` plus the agreed identity or configuration fields from live module state.
Implement a reusable aggregate-status helper that reduces component statuses with `unhealthy > degraded > ok`, even though the current builders will all emit `ok` in this checkout.

Add a dedicated `/v1/health` payload helper that starts from the legacy health fields and appends the flat `version`, float `uptime_seconds`, and `components` contract using the new shared helpers instead of route-local literals.

Sanity-check the resulting helper boundary so this step stays scoped to shared health assembly in `backend/server.py`: no router wiring yet, no `/health` or `/healthz` payload changes, and no live dependency probes or test-only reset behavior introduced.
